dashboard.title: A Lifetime of Dedication
dashboard.subtitle: For more than five decades, horsemanship has been more than a sport—it has been a way of life and a continuous journey of learning, dedication, and achievement. The story began in Bahrain during the 1970s, and over the years, accumulated experience evolved into a rich legacy spanning horse training, stable management, endurance riding, and equestrian development. Between past and present, one goal has remained unchanged: serving the horse, developing riders, and representing the nation with pride on both local and international stages. Today, this journey stands as part of the enduring history of equestrian excellence in the region and beyond.
dashboard.yearsExperienceDesc: More than five decades dedicated to horsemanship.
dashboard.milestonesDesc: A journey filled with remarkable achievements and memories.
dashboard.intlCertsDesc: Training and qualifications from global institutions.
dashboard.regionalCertsDesc: Accredited expertise from leading regional organizations.
dashboard.royalEventsDesc: Representing Bahrain in prestigious equestrian events.
dashboard.championshipsDesc: Achievements earned across local and international arenas.
dashboard.countriesDesc: Traveling worldwide to gain and share knowledge.
dashboard.horsesTrainedDesc: Developing and preparing hundreds of horses over the years.

timeline.title: Interactive Legacy Timeline
timeline.subtitle: From the 1970s to the present day — a journey of passion and experience that helped shape a lasting legacy across generations of horsemanship and endurance.

tl-1970s-01.title: 1970s – Where the Journey Began
tl-1970s-01.description: The story began with a simple passion for horses—riding, caring, and learning. What started in Bahrain during the 1970s would later grow into a lifelong legacy in horsemanship and endurance.
tl-1970s-01.imageAlt: Archive photographs — early equestrian years in Bahrain, 1970s

tl-1970s-02.title: Community Service & Charity Initiatives
tl-1970s-02.description: As proud Bahrainis, giving back to the community has always been part of the journey. Supporting charitable organizations and participating in humanitarian initiatives reflected a commitment to serving society beyond the world of horses.
tl-1970s-02.imageAlt: Archive photographs — 1975 charity horse trail and community horsemanship in Bahrain

tl-1980s-01.title: 1980s – Apprentice Jockey
tl-1980s-01.description: The early 1980s marked the transition from passion to commitment, as the Apprentice Jockey license represented the first official step in a journey that would span decades of learning, experience, and achievement.
tl-1980s-01.quote: Every great journey begins with a first step.
tl-1980s-01.imageAlt: Bahrain Equestrian and Horse Racing Club apprentice jockey licence, 1980–1981

tl-1980s-02.title: Pioneering the Cowboy Lifestyle in Bahrain
tl-1980s-02.description: In 1984, he was among the first to introduce Western horsemanship and cowboy culture to Bahrain, owning a complete set of authentic cowboy equipment at a time when it was rarely seen in the region.
tl-1980s-02.imageAlt: Archive photographs — pioneering Western cowboy horsemanship in Saar, Bahrain, 1984

tl-1990s-01.title: International Training Camps
tl-1990s-01.description: The 1990s marked the beginning of a new chapter of growth and learning through international training camps, starting in Switzerland and expanding across Europe. These experiences provided valuable exposure to advanced horsemanship practices and international expertise.
tl-1990s-01.quote: True experience begins beyond familiar horizons.
tl-1990s-01.imageAlt: Archive photographs — international equestrian training camps in Switzerland and Europe, 1990s

tl-1990s-02.title: Bringing International Expertise to Bahrain
tl-1990s-02.description: Building on years of international experience, the next milestone was introducing global equestrian standards to Bahrain through the establishment of one of the country's earliest specialized riding and show-jumping centers. Al Zubara became the starting point of this vision, with the center playing a pioneering role in organizing competitions and developing future generations of riders according to international standards.
tl-1990s-02.imageAlt: Archive photographs and press clippings — Al Zubara equestrian center and show-jumping competitions in Bahrain, 1990s

tl-1990s-03.title: Saar Riding School
tl-1990s-03.description: The Saar Riding School marked a significant milestone in the development of equestrian sports in Bahrain. Established according to international standards, it featured dedicated facilities for riding instruction, show jumping, and endurance training. The school also attracted renowned international experts and trainers, including leading figures in show-jumping education. Over the years, it helped develop many riders who have become recognized names in Bahrain’s equestrian community.
tl-1990s-03.imageAlt: Archive photographs and press — Saar Riding School competitions and training in Bahrain, 1998

tl-2000s-01.title: Joining the Bahrain Royal Team
tl-2000s-01.description: This decade marked the beginning of a new chapter with the appointment as a trainer for the Bahrain Royal Team. It was the start of a journey dedicated to representing the Kingdom of Bahrain and contributing to the advancement of equestrian and endurance sports at the highest level, both locally and internationally.
tl-2000s-01.quote: Representing your nation is an honor; building success is a responsibility.
tl-2000s-01.imageAlt: Archive photographs — joining the Bahrain Royal Endurance Team as a trainer, 2000s

tl-2000s-02.title: A New Era of Success with the Royal Team
tl-2000s-02.description: This period marked the beginning of a remarkable journey of achievements with the Bahrain Royal Team, both locally and internationally. Abdulrahman Al Bastaki established himself as one of the leading figures in endurance riding, combining the skills of an accomplished rider with the expertise of a respected trainer, earning recognition across regional and international competitions.
tl-2000s-02.quote: True greatness is measured not by a single race, but by a lifetime of achievements.
tl-2000s-02.imageAlt: Archive photographs — Bahrain Royal Team endurance competitions and training, 2005

tl-2010s-01.title: A Decade of Golden Achievements
tl-2010s-01.description: This journey was not defined solely by experience, training, and participation—it was measured by success. Throughout this decade, Abdulrahman Al Bastaki stood on numerous podiums across local and international competitions, earning recognition as both a rider and a trainer, and cementing his reputation among the leading figures in endurance sport.
tl-2010s-01.quote: Gold is not the goal of the journey, but the result of years of dedication.
tl-2010s-01.imageAlt: Archive photographs — podium victories and golden achievements in endurance, 2010s

tl-2010s-02.title: Flat Racing & Knowledge Exchange
tl-2010s-02.description: During this period, Abdulrahman Al Bastaki maintained an active presence in flat racing events and equestrian gatherings. His participation provided valuable opportunities to exchange knowledge and experience with leading trainers and riders from different countries, further enriching his expertise and broadening his understanding of the sport.
tl-2010s-02.quote: Success begins with continuous learning and the exchange of experience.
tl-2010s-02.imageAlt: Archive photographs — flat racing and knowledge exchange at international venues, 2014

tl-2010s-03.title: Royal Trust in Horse Training Excellence
tl-2010s-03.description: Abdulrahman Al Bastaki's role extended far beyond training and international participation. He earned the trust of the Royal Court to prepare and train horses ridden by His Majesty King Hamad bin Isa Al Khalifa, King of the Kingdom of Bahrain. Through his extensive experience and exceptional capabilities, he supervised the training of several royal horses, including “Haji Baik,” which was gifted by President Vladimir Putin to His Majesty King Hamad bin Isa Al Khalifa in 2016.
tl-2010s-03.quote: Royal trust is built on expertise, dedication, and results.
tl-2010s-03.imageAlt: Archive photographs — royal horse training and the presentation of Haji Baik, 2016

tl-2020s-01.title: Sharing Knowledge & Building Future Generations
tl-2020s-01.description: Abdulrahman Al Bastaki's journey has extended far beyond competition and sporting achievements. Throughout his career, he has traveled to numerous countries and leading equestrian centers, not for tourism alone, but to gain knowledge, develop his skills, and learn from international best practices. He continues to bring this expertise back to Bahrain, contributing to the growth of equestrian sport and helping develop future generations of riders.
tl-2020s-01.quote: The pursuit of knowledge never ends, and true experience is measured by the ability to share it with others.
tl-2020s-01.imageAlt: Archive photographs — sharing equestrian knowledge and building future generations worldwide

tl-2020s-02.title: Training Royal and Distinguished Riders
tl-2020s-02.description: Abdulrahman Al Bastaki had the honor of training members of the royal family, including sons of His Highness Sheikh Khalid bin Hamad Al Khalifa and His Highness Sheikh Nasser bin Hamad Al Khalifa, as well as a number of distinguished officials and prominent figures. This reflects the trust he has earned throughout his career through his expertise, dedication, and ability to develop riders to the highest professional standards.
tl-2020s-02.quote: Trust is earned through experience and strengthened through achievement.
tl-2020s-02.imageAlt: Archive photograph — training royal and distinguished young riders in Bahrain, 2022

tl-2020s-03.title: A Continuing Legacy of Horsemanship
tl-2020s-03.description:
Abdulrahman Al Bastaki continues to serve Bahraini equestrian sport as a trainer with the Royal Team at Al Fursan Stables, building upon decades of experience and dedication. He believes that Bahrain has long been, and remains, one of the leading nations in horsemanship and equestrian excellence, a vision established by His Majesty King Hamad bin Isa Al Khalifa, the Kingdom of Bahrain’s foremost horseman and leader.

The sport has continued to flourish under the leadership of His Highness Sheikh Nasser bin Hamad Al Khalifa, His Highness Sheikh Khalid bin Hamad Al Khalifa, and Her Highness Shaikha Noora bint Hamad Al Khalifa, whose efforts have strengthened Bahrain’s position on the international stage. The continued support of His Royal Highness Prince Salman bin Hamad Al Khalifa, Crown Prince and Prime Minister, for horse racing and equestrian sports further reflects the deep-rooted connection between Bahrain’s people and their equestrian heritage.
tl-2020s-03.quote: In Bahrain, horsemanship is more than a sport—it is a national legacy and a living culture passed through generations.
tl-2020s-03.imageAlt: Archive portrait — a continuing legacy of horsemanship at Al Fursan Stables

legacy.title: Moments That Shaped the Journey
legacy.subtitle: This section showcases a collection of memorable photographs featuring Abdulrahman Al Bastaki alongside royal, sporting, and distinguished personalities throughout his career. Each image reflects a story of trust, achievement, and a shared passion for equestrian excellence.

story-01.title: The Inspiring Leader & First Horseman
story-01.subtitle: His Majesty King Hamad bin Isa Al Khalifa
story-01.description: Abdulrahman Al Bastaki believes that His Majesty King Hamad bin Isa Al Khalifa is the inspiring leader and foremost horseman who established the vision that shaped Bahrain’s modern equestrian success. Through his unwavering support for Bahraini youth and his commitment to preserving the nation’s equestrian heritage, Bahrain has become one of the leading countries in the world of horsemanship and endurance sport. Al Bastaki affirms that, by the grace of God and the leadership of His Majesty, Bahrain has achieved remarkable progress and excellence across all sectors.
story-01.quote: Great nations are built upon vision, and great generations are inspired by leadership.
story-01.imageAlt: His Majesty King Hamad bin Isa Al Khalifa walking with companions — royal equestrian heritage archive

story-02.title: A Leader of Achievement and Confidence
story-02.subtitle: His Royal Highness Prince Salman bin Hamad Al Khalifa
story-02.description:
Abdulrahman Al Bastaki views His Royal Highness Prince Salman bin Hamad Al Khalifa, Crown Prince and Prime Minister, as an inspiring leader who has instilled confidence and ambition in the hearts of Bahrainis. Through his vision, leadership, and unwavering support, progress has become a defining characteristic of the Kingdom’s journey.

Al Bastaki believes that no national achievement or international success is complete without the impact of His Royal Highness’s guidance and support, which continue to empower Bahrain’s growth and prosperity.
story-02.quote: Great achievements begin with leaders who believe in their people and invest in their future.
story-02.imageAlt: His Royal Highness Prince Salman bin Hamad Al Khalifa with companions at a national sporting event

story-03.title: Leaders Who Inspired a Generation
story-03.description: According to Al Bastaki, both leaders share a deep belief in the potential of Bahraini youth, a belief that continues to drive achievement and excellence across the Kingdom.
story-03.quote: When leaders believe in their youth, achievement becomes a national culture.
story-03.imageAlt: His Highness Sheikh Nasser bin Hamad Al Khalifa and His Highness Sheikh Khalid bin Hamad Al Khalifa at a ceremonial recognition — youth and sporting heritage archive

nasser.name: His Highness Sheikh Nasser bin Hamad Al Khalifa
nasser.role:
Representative of His Majesty the King for Humanitarian Work and Youth Affairs
National Security Advisor
nasser.description: Abdulrahman Al Bastaki believes that His Highness Sheikh Nasser bin Hamad Al Khalifa embodies determination, ambition, and leadership. From an early age, he forged his path toward international success, becoming a world champion and a role model for Bahraini youth. His competitive spirit and perseverance have inspired generations of athletes and equestrians across the Kingdom.

khalid.name: His Highness Sheikh Khalid bin Hamad Al Khalifa
khalid.role:
First Deputy Chairman of the Supreme Council for Youth and Sports
Chairman of the General Sports Authority
President of the Bahrain Olympic Committee
khalid.description: Al Bastaki also regards His Highness Sheikh Khalid bin Hamad Al Khalifa as a symbol of empowerment and youth development. Through his continuous support for sports and young talents, he has created opportunities for Bahrainis to excel and compete on the highest levels.

story-04.title: A Key Supporter of the Journey
story-04.subtitle: His Highness Sheikh Abdullah bin Isa Al Khalifa
story-04.description:
Abdulrahman Al Bastaki acknowledges the significant role played by His Highness Sheikh Abdullah bin Isa Al Khalifa in supporting endurance riding in the Kingdom of Bahrain since the early 2000s. His commitment to the development of the Royal Team and the sport itself contributed greatly to strengthening Bahrain's position in endurance riding.

Al Bastaki believes that this continued support helped establish the foundations for many of Bahrain's achievements, creating an environment where riders and horses could develop and compete at the highest levels.
story-04.quote: Great achievements begin with leaders who believe in building future champions.
story-04.imageAlt: His Highness Sheikh Abdullah bin Isa Al Khalifa with the endurance team at a ceremonial presentation — royal equestrian archive

story-05.title: His Late Majesty Sultan Qaboos bin Said
story-05.subtitle: A Leader Who Strengthened Heritage and Brotherhood
story-05.description:
Abdulrahman Al Bastaki fondly recalls the visit of the late Sultan Qaboos bin Said to the Kingdom of Bahrain in the early 2000s, where he attended several equestrian and heritage events that reflected the strong and historic ties between Bahrain and the Sultanate of Oman.

Al Bastaki believes that Sultan Qaboos and his accompanying delegation played a significant role in supporting equestrian and endurance sports, helping to strengthen their presence at both local and regional levels through cooperation, knowledge exchange, and the preservation of Gulf heritage.

These visits left a lasting impact on the equestrian community and further strengthened the cultural and sporting relations between the two brotherly nations, reflecting the shared values and traditions of the Gulf region.
story-05.quote: Equestrian sport remains a bridge that unites Gulf nations through heritage, respect, and shared values.
story-05.imageAlt: His Late Majesty Sultan Qaboos bin Said with His Majesty King Hamad bin Isa Al Khalifa at a formal Gulf gathering — brotherhood and heritage archive

story-06.title: Leaders and Horsemen Who Supported Equestrian Sport
story-06.description: He emphasises that the support provided by Arab leaders has played a significant role in advancing these traditional sports and preserving them as an important part of the region’s history, identity, and shared culture.
story-06.quote: When leaders support equestrian sport, the heritage of the horse continues to inspire generations.
story-06.imageAlt: His Highness Sheikh Mohammed bin Rashid Al Maktoum and His Majesty King Abdullah II with His Majesty King Hamad at an international endurance championship — Arab equestrian leadership archive

maktoum.name: His Highness Sheikh Mohammed bin Rashid Al Maktoum
maktoum.role: Vice President and Prime Minister of the United Arab Emirates, Ruler of Dubai
maktoum.description:
Abdulrahman Al Bastaki regards His Highness Sheikh Mohammed bin Rashid Al Maktoum, widely known as the Arabian Horseman, as one of the most prominent supporters of horse racing, endurance riding, and equestrian sport in the Arab world.

Through his deep passion for horses and his ambitious vision, His Highness has contributed significantly to the development of these disciplines, strengthened their international presence, and supported major races and championships that have positioned the region as a leading global destination for equestrian sport.

His achievements and distinguished journey in horsemanship have also inspired generations of riders, while his continuous support has reinforced the horse’s important place within Arab identity, heritage, and culture.

abdullah.name: His Majesty King Abdullah II bin Al Hussein
abdullah.role: King of the Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan
abdullah.description:
Abdulrahman Al Bastaki also values the role of His Majesty King Abdullah II bin Al Hussein in supporting horse racing and equestrian sport, as well as his commitment to preserving this deeply rooted Arab heritage and passing it on to future generations.

Al Bastaki believes that His Majesty’s interest in sporting and heritage activities has encouraged riders, supported equestrian events, and strengthened the position of horsemanship at both local and regional levels.

gallery.title: Moments Preserved Through Time
gallery.subtitle: Royal encounters, achievements, and milestones that shaped an extraordinary journey.

newspapers.title: Featured Through the Years
newspapers.subtitle: Press coverage and publications documenting key moments of the journey.

certificates.title: Awards & Certificates of Excellence
certificates.subtitle: Honors and certifications reflecting a distinguished journey of achievement.

royalConnections.title: Words of Gratitude and Loyalty
royalConnections.statement:
Abdulrahman Al Bastaki extends his sincere gratitude and appreciation to the wise leadership of the Kingdom of Bahrain, led by His Majesty King Hamad bin Isa Al Khalifa, for the continuous support and attention given to equestrian sports, which have helped establish Bahrain as one of the region’s leading nations in horsemanship and endurance riding.

He also expresses his appreciation for the ongoing support of Their Highnesses and distinguished leaders, whose encouragement has played a vital role in developing the sport and empowering Bahraini riders to achieve success on regional and international stages.
royalConnections.quote: Every achievement is the result of a leadership that believes in its people and a nation that has made excellence a way of life and horsemanship a source of pride.
royalConnections.imageAlt: His Majesty King Hamad bin Isa Al Khalifa greeting endurance riders — royal patronage archive

contact.title: Digital Archive Development
contact.subtitle:
This digital archive was designed, developed, and implemented by the Information Technology Team at PERMANENT INTEGRATION TRADING W.L.L (PMI IT Solutions), reflecting the company's commitment to preserving national achievements and documenting Bahrain’s rich heritage for future generations.

PMI IT Solutions takes pride in supporting Bahraini sports, national talents, and distinguished figures across various sectors through innovative digital solutions and technology platforms that help showcase success stories and achievements that represent the Kingdom of Bahrain and its people.
contact.quote: We believe technology is not merely a tool, but a bridge that preserves heritage, empowers talent, and tells the stories of success for generations to come.

footer.biography:
For more than five decades, Abdulrahman Al Bastaki has dedicated his life to the world of horses and equestrian sport, combining practical expertise with a lifelong passion rooted in authenticity and commitment. Throughout his distinguished journey, he has contributed to the training and development of horses and riders while witnessing and supporting the growth of endurance riding in Bahrain and the region.

This platform reflects part of that remarkable journey, documenting decades of achievements, experiences, and memories that have helped shape an enduring legacy in the world of horsemanship.
footer.developer: Designed & developed by PMI IT Solutions.

introModal.title: Why "Uncle Tim"?
introModal.imageAlt: Archive photograph — the story behind the name Uncle Tim
introModal.p1: The nickname "Uncle Tim" was given to Abdulrahman Al Bastaki by the team at Waterstock House Training Centre in the United Kingdom during his European training camps in 1990.
introModal.p2: The name was a reflection of his exceptional horsemanship, natural ability to train and handle horses, and the trust he was able to build with them from an early age.
introModal.p3: Over time, "Uncle Tim" became a respected name associated with experience, patience, leadership, and a deep understanding of horses, remaining part of his identity throughout decades of achievements in the equestrian world.
